message:
test(ft-xbnl0.2.4): broadcast mid-flight cancel via select-race pattern (tick 434)

Extends the ticks 432/433 probe to broadcast_recv_with_cx. Same test
shape as the mpsc/oneshot mid-flight tests.

**Outcome**: watcher branch fires consistently — confirms the same
gap applies to broadcast receivers. Asupersync's broadcast recv does
NOT register a cx-cancel-waker for already-suspended subscribers.

Broadcast is especially operationally important: it underpins the
event fanout layer in `events.rs` + `ipc.rs`, so cx-cancel mid-flight
on a broadcast subscriber wouldn't be observed without the
select-race pattern.

Paired primitive × cancel-timing matrix now generalised across three
channel types:

| Primitive               | Pre-cancel | Mid-flight waker | Caller pattern |
|-------------------------|------------|------------------|----------------|
| mpsc::Receiver::recv    | ✓ tick 422 | ✗                | tick 432       |
| oneshot_recv_with_cx    | ✓ tick 419 | ✗                | tick 433       |
| broadcast_recv_with_cx  | ✓ tick 420 | ✗                | tick 434       |

All three share the same asupersync design: per-poll
`cx.checkpoint()` short-circuit (fires on pre-cancel + any re-poll
triggered externally) but no cx-cancel-waker registration. Callers
needing mid-flight cancel observability must wrap in a
`futures::future::select` against a poll-sleep watcher (same shape
as tick 387's `DistributedHttpClient::race_with_cx_cancel` fix for
ft-l9mxa).

Observed locally: 0.13 s wall — cancel at 100 ms, watcher observes
within 50 ms poll cycle.

Check script Run 6/6 filter `broadcast_recv_with_cx` already catches
the new test. Run 6 grows 18 → 19. Total: 105 → 106.

Full check script passes locally via fork bypass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
